source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Basic common-emitter amplifier circuit is shown in figure 31. Assume the transistor has \(\b_x0008_eta = 100 \), \( r_{bb'} = 200 \Omega \), \( U_{BEQ} = 0.6 V \), and \( C_1 \), \( C_2 \) are large enough. It is known that \( V_{CC} = 10 V \), \( R_b = 470 K \Omega \), \( R_C = R_L = 3 K \Omega \). The value of the output resistance \( R_o \) is ( )k\Omega.

The problem provides several electrical parameters (beta, r_bb', U_BEQ, V_CC, R_b, R_C, R_L, capacitor assumptions), implying some parameter filtering or combination rather than direct lookup.
The target is a derived numeric quantity, not a direct reading from the image, so at least modest multi-step reasoning is involved.
